source_intake_record.raw_question_text: AgNO_3 (aq.) was added to an aqueous KCl solution gradually and the conductivity of the solution was measured. The plot of conductance ($\Lambda$) versus the volume of AgNO_3 is <image_1>
extraction_notes: answer field is option letter 'D', mapped to choice text '(S)'
image field contains a PIL image placeholder rather than a file path or URL

image_dependency: question asks 'Which plot' matches the experiment, so the target options are graphical
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the problem is not solvable from text alone
choices are plot labels '(P)/(Q)/(R)/(S)', implying the decisive content is in the image
multi_step: solving requires reasoning about how conductance changes as AgNO3 is gradually added to KCl, not simple lookup
the task then requires mapping that inferred trend to one of four plotted options
single-best-choice format limits complexity, so multi-step demand is moderate rather than very high
verifiability: answer is a discrete option label '(S)', so correctness is easy to check against the key
there are four explicit choices, which supports objective evaluation
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true and answer_present=true reduce ambiguity about available supervision

retained original answer and option labels without semantic changes
